data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "The collapse was triggered by a cascading failure of the international bronze supply chain, where the loss of a single critical tin source caused a systemic economic depression that delegitimized palace authority.", "mechanism": "Supply-side shock leading to currency debasement (in kind) and loss of elite monopoly on weaponry.", "scope": {"covers": ["Mycenaean Greece", "Hittite Anatolia", "Ugarit"], "excludes": ["Egypt", "Cyprus"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Archaeological evidence of abundant tin stockpiles in destroyed palace layers dated post-1200 BC", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "Relies on the high interdependence of the Late Bronze Age gift-exchange economy; explains simultaneity via trade networks rather than migration."}
